untranslatable_reason: Signal requires Johansen cointegration test and portfolio-level statistical arbitrage logic that cannot be expressed using only the allowed price-based indicators (close, sma, rsi, atr, bb, macd, adx, stochastic, donchian, pivots, volume, returns). Cointegration analysis requires multivariate time-series modeling across multiple assets simultaneously, which is beyond the scope of single-asset technical indicator comparisons. The spread calculation between a dynamically selected stock portfolio and SPY also requires cross-asset correlation tracking not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: This is a pairs/cointegration trading strategy that requires statistical testing (Johansen test) to identify which SPY constituent stocks cointegrate with the ETF, then forms a portfolio and trades the spread. The core logic (equal-weight portfolio selection based on cointegration probability, spread mean-reversion) cannot be reduced to standard technical indicators. A proper implementation would require a custom cointegration module outside the standard backtest engine framework. The placeholder entry/exit uses price crossing SMA as a rough directional proxy only.
